steps.title: Apply the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: The exponent $\frac{1}{2}$ converts directly to a square root.

steps.title: Convert the expression
steps.content:
Apply this rule to $(39y)^{\frac{1}{2}}$. The exponent applies to the entire quantity $(39y)$:

$$(39y)^{\frac{1}{2}} = \sqrt{39y}$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
